title: All fields in one repeating logical record share a single bind::oc:itemgroup
description: OC4 documentation: 'In the bind::oc:itemgroup column, you are using repeating groups, which must be kept separate, it is ideal to keep items in the same group. These groups do not have to correspond to the begin/end groups. Keeping items in the same group allows for better data visualization in Insight because participant data is displayed on tables by item group, not by form.' The visual begin group / end group markers don't have to match the bind::oc:itemgroup data-grouping value. DEFAULT: within a repeating logical record (one AE, one CM, one MH event), all data fields share a single bind::oc:itemgroup value matching the CDASH domain code (AE, CM, MH, DV, DD, etc.) — regardless of how many begin group / end group blocks exist for visual organization. Override: multiple genuinely distinct sub-records within one form (rare but legitimate — e.g. a form capturing both header-level and line-item-level data with independent repeat semantics).
rationale: Insight's tabular data visualization groups participant data by item group, not by form. If a repeating AE-form record has fields split across multiple itemgroups (one per visual group), Insight presents the data as multiple unrelated row sets instead of one logical AE event. Keeping all fields in one logical record under a single itemgroup makes the data render correctly in Insight tables and in downstream exports. Visual organization (begin group / end group) can still be used for screen layout — the two concerns are independent.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} has repeating logical records (has_repeating_group=true). Verify ALL data fields within the repeating record share a single bind__oc_itemgroup value matching the CDASH domain code (${form.cdash_domain}). Visual begin group / end group can subdivide the layout; the itemgroup data-grouping must NOT. Exception: multiple genuinely distinct sub-records (rare).

effect.soft: For forms with has_repeating_group=true: enforce that all data-bearing fields within the repeating section share one itemgroup value, equal to form.cdash_domain (e.g. AE, CM, MH, DV, DD). The check requires resolving the field's position relative to the begin_group/end_group boundaries that mark the repeating record — handled in soft. Surface in conventions_applied.itemgroup_keep_together.{repeating_logical_records, repeating_records_consistent, deviations}.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §16 during Phase B.1d. Complements OC-2 (field_metadata.itemgroup_mandatory_on_data_rows) — OC-2 says 'every data row must have an itemgroup populated'; §16 says 'all rows in one repeating logical record share the SAME itemgroup value'. Different rules, both apply. Hybrid because the structural check requires identifying which rows are 'within the repeating section' — a survey-row-position query that isn't a clean DSL primitive yet. A future 'field.within_repeating_section' computed attribute would promote this to structured.
